name: The Weeping Depths
subtitle: The sea remembers everything it swallows
theme: Perseverance / Hidden truths / The cost of power

shard.name: Seal Fragment of Water
shard.description: A deep blue crystal that weeps light continuously. It hums with the memory of countless tides.

scenes.narration: The Oracle's third marker leads to the coast of Aethoria's sunken west — a region that drowned when Valdris first shattered the Water Seal.
Nilou stands at the shoreline, watching the water with a strange expression — recognition, almost.

dialogue.speaker: Lulu
dialogue.text: In my homeland, the sacred pools respond to dance. This water feels different. Grief-soaked.

dialogue.speaker: Tao
dialogue.text: Grief-soaked water. That is poetic. I might use that in a poem, if you do not mind.

dialogue.speaker: Lulu
dialogue.text: It is yours. Though I hope you give it a more cheerful ending than the sea did.

scenes.narration: Xiao crouches at the edge, scanning beneath the surface. Shadow-forms move in the depths — slow, circling.

dialogue.speaker: Rei
dialogue.text: The Kraken. Ancient. Pre-dates Valdris. He corrupted it when he broke the Seal — turned a guardian into a warden.

dialogue.speaker: Aya
dialogue.text: A creature once protector of these waters, now their jailer. There is a particular sadness in that.

dialogue.speaker: Tao
dialogue.text: A sad fate. But also very large tentacles. Let us have a plan.

chapters.title: Into the Scorched Spiral
chapters.map_hint: Cross the Ember Wastes — the drowned coast and the Kraken lie beyond the scorched spiral.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The path to the sunken coast cuts through the Ember Wastes — a scarred landscape where even the rock burns. Shadow-corrupted creatures stalk between the cinders.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Rei
pre_dialogue.text: The Kraken's karmic weight grows stronger ahead. We are close.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Tao
pre_dialogue.text: Hot, dangerous, and full of things that want us dead. Perfectly normal Tuesday. Move.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Ember Wastes give way to the sunken coast — a shoreline drowned in grief-soaked water. And beneath the surface, something immense circles.

post_dialogue.speaker: Lulu
post_dialogue.text: This water feels different. Grief-soaked, like it remembers what it lost.

post_dialogue.speaker: Rei
post_dialogue.text: The Kraken does not want to fight. The shadow compels it. Remember that — and make it quick.

post_dialogue.speaker: Tao
post_dialogue.text: It deserves that much. Let us do what we came to do.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Kraken rises from the depths. Ancient. Immense. Its corruption pulses with the captured Seal Fragment, and it comes for them with blind fury.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Rei
pre_dialogue.text: It does not want this fight. But it has no choice. That makes it more dangerous.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Lulu
pre_dialogue.text: Then we end this quickly. For its sake.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Tao
pre_dialogue.text: For its sake, and for ours. Let us do what we came here to do.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Kraken falls. As its shadow-form dissolves, the water Seal Fragment rises from the depths — clear and cold and weeping light.

post_dialogue.speaker: Rei
post_dialogue.text: It is gone now. The karma is lifting. It can finally rest.

post_dialogue.speaker: Lulu
post_dialogue.text: I am glad it is free. I just wish we could have done it without the fighting.

post_dialogue.speaker: Aya
post_dialogue.text: Sometimes the only kindness left is the one that ends suffering. Not comfortable — but true.

post_dialogue.speaker: Tao
post_dialogue.text: ...I have seen enough to know she is right. Three Fragments down. One more before we truly face him.

dialogue.text: As the water settles, a figure descends from above — a dragoon knight, his armor marked with the sigil of a fallen kingdom. His lance glows with aerial energy as he lands beside them.

dialogue.speaker: Drake
dialogue.text: I am Drake, Dragoon of the Sky. I felt the Fragment's freedom from above — the sky itself sang with it. A barrier I did not know existed has lifted.

dialogue.speaker: Ria
dialogue.text: Drake? From that fallen kingdom? That kingdom fell ages ago in my world. How is it you stand here?

dialogue.speaker: Drake
dialogue.text: I do not know. But I was bound by a curse I could not break. Now, feeling that curse shatter — I followed the sensation here. To help.

dialogue.speaker: Rei
dialogue.text: A warrior chained by his own oaths, freed by the breaking of chains elsewhere. I understand that more than most.

dialogue.speaker: Aya
dialogue.text: Then let us bind ourselves together — not by curse, but by choice. Will you stand with us?

dialogue.speaker: Drake
dialogue.text: I will. My lance answers to a new oath now: the oath to see darkness broken.
